event: Agrippina Landing at Brundisium with the Ashes of Germanicus
symbols: veiled Agrippina bearing the urn — devoted marital love and dignified grief, a model of Roman virtus
crowds meeting her at the harbor — public mourning for a beloved leader, collective grief
classical architecture and drapery — the ordered world of Roman civilization
funerary urn — the mortal remains of earthly glory, the transience of human life and power
description: A large neoclassical history painting showing a solemn procession at a Roman port. A veiled woman in white — Agrippina — walks at the center, bearing or accompanying a funerary urn. She is surrounded by attendants and mourners in classical dress. Roman citizens line the way, many with bowed heads or gestures of grief. Classical architecture frames the scene. The palette is restrained with muted earth tones, white, and grey, befitting the funerary subject.
